action_items.task: Travel directly to and from Saint Jude Medical Center without making any stops.
action_items.context: Temporary emergency travel pass issued to visit hospitalized mother.

action_items.task: Provide proof of hospital visit (visitor pass or parking receipt) at the next appointment.
action_items.context: To verify compliance with the temporary travel pass to Saint Jude Medical Center.

action_items.task: Call PO for approval before visiting the hospital again this weekend or if mother is transferred to a different facility.
action_items.context: To ensure any future emergency travel is properly authorized and documented.

action_items.task: Wait for instructions from PO or call the on-call number if mother is transferred and it's time to leave.
action_items.context: Backup plan if the mother is moved to a rehabilitation center and the client cannot reach the primary PO.

action_items.task: Be home for the BI Monitoring inspection.
action_items.context: BI Monitoring needs to verify the new outlet in the kitchen and test signal strength.

action_items.task: Call the police if Hector returns and causes any problems.
action_items.context: Safety protocol following Hector moving out of the residence.

critical_updates.details: Client's mother, Elena Gonzalez, suffered a stroke and was hospitalized at Saint Jude Medical Center.
Hector moved out of the residence on Sunday. The client's daughter and two grandsons (ages 4 and 6) have moved in. The daughter is assisting with sewing and purchasing food.
The ankle monitor base station was moved to the kitchen because the bedroom breaker is broken. The unit has maintained power successfully since the move.

Client is currently working full-time doing sewing alterations to cover rent after Hector's departure. She submitted a food sales permit application downtown last Thursday to sell tamales and is awaiting approval (expected in ~2 weeks).
Client reports morning stiffness in her hands due to arthritis; she is managing the pain with warm water and Tylenol in order to continue sewing.

Client was granted a temporary emergency travel pass for today (1:30 PM - 6:30 PM) to visit her mother at the hospital. BI Monitoring is scheduled for an inspection on Friday at 9:00 AM. No police contact reported.
Client reports no alcohol or drug use.

next_episode_context: Maria is currently working full-time from home doing sewing alterations after Hector moved out. Her daughter and two grandsons (ages 4 and 6) have moved in and are helping with food and household tasks. She has applied for a food sales permit to sell tamales legally and expects a response in about two weeks. Her mother recently suffered a stroke and is hospitalized at Saint Jude Medical Center; Maria was issued a temporary travel pass for visitation today. A BI Monitoring inspection of her home base station (recently moved to the kitchen) is scheduled for Friday morning. No legal or substance issues reported.
